article_title: Kīlauea Episode 43 Sets Fountain Height Record as Ash Reaches Communities 50 Miles Away

content_preview.title: Kīlauea Episode 43 Sets Fountain Height Record as Ash Reaches Communities 50 Miles Away
content_preview.summary: Kīlauea's 43rd eruptive episode on March 10 produced record 540-meter lava fountains and sent ash across communities 80 kilometers away, prompting closures and flight cancellations as USGS forecasts Episode 44 for late March.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Kīlauea volcano's 43rd eruptive episode sent lava fountains to a record 540 meters (1,770 feet) on March 10, 2026, the [highest of the current eruption cycle that began in December 2024](https://www.usgs.gov/observatories/hvo/news/volcano-watch-episode-43-new-fountain-height-record-and-tephra-fallout). The nine-hour event ejected an estimated 12 million cubic meters (16 million cubic yards) of lava from two vents on the western floor of Halemaʻumaʻu crater, while volcanic ash and te...

editor_notes:
Excellent volcanology news article with strong sourcing from USGS, NASA, and peer-reviewed research. The summary accurately captures the key facts. The article effectively combines event reporting with advances in eruption forecasting technology. Word count (748) is appropriate for News category.

source_verification:
- usgs.gov (chronology): CONFIRMED. Corroborates eruption onset at 9:17am HST March 10, north and south vents, fountain heights exceeding 100m by 10am, 300m by 10:50am, 400m by 11am, eruption end at ~6:21pm HST, 33.7 microradians deflationary tilt.
- usgs.gov (volcano watch): CONFIRMED. Confirms 540m record fountain height, 200+ tephra reports, fluoride levels 0.06-0.3 ppm in catchment water, Episode 44 forecast window March 28-April 14.
- science.nasa.gov: CONFIRMED. Confirms 30,000ft eruption column, ash reaching 50 miles/80km, 12 million cubic meters of lava, 90m cumulative crater floor rise, Landsat 9 thermal imagery. Note: the specific eruption column height timeline (10,000ft by 10:10am, 20,000ft by 11:30am) may come from USGS source rather than NASA.
- sciencedaily.com: CONFIRMED. Confirms Jerk method, 92% detection rate at Piton de la Fournaise 2014-2023, single broadband seismometer, nanometer-scale motions, up to 8.5 hours warning, IPGP and GFZ researchers, Nature Communications publication.
- caltech.edu: CONFIRMED. Confirms DAS fiber-optic monitoring on Reykjanes Peninsula, 30 min to hours advance notice, Zhongwen Zhan, published in Science, millimeter-scale resolution, telecommunications infrastructure reuse.
